body_txt: You think Never Bring a Knife to a Gun Fight always applies, right? Not necessarily. At times, either the knife-wielder is just so good, or the gun-wielder is just so bad , that the knife-wielder actually has the upper hand here. This can also be due to Arbitrary Weapon Range — once the knife-wielder gets within the gun-wielder's minimum range, the gun-wielder cannot attack them effectively. Truth in Television in certain circumstances, as the Real Life section notes. Not to be confused with guns in a knife fight being used as a metaphor for Combat Pragmatist . Subtrope of Implausible Fencing Powers and Heroes Prefer Swords . Contrast Never Bring a Knife to a Gun Fight . If the gun user is defeated by someone with no weapons at all, it's a case of Never Bring a Knife to a Fist Fight . Related to Guns Are Worthless . May involve Parrying Bullets on part of the knife-wielder.
